papers.title: Behistun Inscription
papers.notes: Old Persian / Elamite / Babylonian trilingual; key for cuneiform decipherment

papers.title: Decipherment of cuneiform
papers.notes: Grotefend 1802 → Rawlinson → Hincks → 1857 Royal Asiatic verification

papers.title: The Earliest Contributions to the Decipherment of Sumerian and Akkadian

mechanism_summary: Cuneiform decipherment: monolingual hypothesis tested against bilingual then trilingual cross-checks. Each language's deciphered chunk narrows the hypothesis space for the next language. The 1857 Royal Asiatic Society blind-comparison test was the convergence verification — 4 scholars independently produced translations that agreed enough to confirm the decipherment had reached a fixed point. Information cascades: each correct symbol-to-sound mapping cascades to narrow plausible mappings for adjacent symbols.
